purpose: Plan a PDF-to-PPT deck using img-gen + Presentation by confirming PDF scope, style, and speaker-notes preference; allocating slide counts by content volume and importance; generating an outline; letting Presentation define image requirements; using img-gen for backgrounds and Presentation-requested image assets; and batching decks over 10 slides.

implementation_technology.img_gen_role: Generate slide background images and any additional image assets requested by Presentation, following Presentation-provided dimensions, style, visual references, and editable-text safe areas.
implementation_technology.presentation_role: Handle all remaining work: plan execution, PPT creation, layout, inserting generated images, editable text and PowerPoint objects, optional speaker notes, page numbering, batch assembly, and merging.
implementation_technology.critical_rule: img-gen only produces backgrounds and Presentation-requested image assets. Do not embed final readable titles, bullet points, conclusions, or main PDF-derived text in img-gen images; add them through Presentation as editable PPT content.

pdf_scope.question: Which part of the PDF should be used to generate the PPT?
pdf_scope.notes: Do not proceed to slide allocation, outline generation, img-gen, or PPT creation until this is known.

ppt_style.question: What PPT style should be used?
ppt_style.usage: Use consistently in the PPT theme, Presentation layout choices, and every img-gen background or Presentation-requested image prompt.

speaker_notes.question: Should speaker notes be generated for the PPT?
speaker_notes.usage: If true, create concise presenter notes for each slide. If false, do not add speaker notes.

style_notes.background_art_direction: Use the confirmed style; default to academic minimalist style when unspecified. Presentation must define image dimensions, safe areas, and visual requirements before img-gen is called.
style_notes.editable_text_overlay_rules: Presentation controls readable safe areas, high contrast, and consistent spacing for editable text boxes.

deck_plan.text_editability_rule: Main PDF-derived or outline-derived text must be editable PowerPoint text added through Presentation; non-essential visuals may be images.
deck_plan.speaker_notes_rule: Generate notes only if preflight_questions.speaker_notes.value is true.

negative_prompt_or_constraints: Do not embed final readable body text into the background image.
Do not rasterize critical titles, bullet points, conclusions, or main PDF-derived text.
Leave clean, high-contrast areas for editable PowerPoint text boxes.

batch_plan.batch_size_rule: 5 slides for design-heavy decks; 6-8 slides for normal academic/report decks; up to 10 slides only when layouts are simple.

batches.visual_style_notes: Use the confirmed deck style consistently across all Presentation layouts, img-gen backgrounds, and Presentation-requested image assets.
batches.speaker_notes_behavior: Generate only if requested in preflight_questions.speaker_notes.value.
batch_checks: titles and main text are editable
img-gen backgrounds are present or intentionally selected
extra img-gen image assets were requested by Presentation
background safe areas support readable editable text
speaker notes presence matches user preference

merge_checks: background and Presentation-requested image asset art direction is consistent
speaker notes presence or absence matches user preference

quality_checks: PDF scope was confirmed before generation
PPT style was confirmed or defaulted to academic minimalist style
speaker-notes preference was confirmed or defaulted to no speaker notes
slide count matches the planned or requested count
slide allocation reflects PDF content volume and importance
every slide has an img-gen background or intentionally selected background
any additional img-gen image assets were requested by Presentation
main PDF-derived or outline-derived text is editable
no critical title/key point/conclusion exists only as flattened image text
speaker notes are present only if requested
visual style is consistent across batches
backgrounds leave enough contrast and whitespace for editable content
recovery_strategy: Reallocate slides if a section is too compressed or too sparse.
Regenerate weak slide backgrounds with larger safe areas or lower visual complexity.
Reduce text density and convert long paragraphs into editable bullets.
Use PDF visuals as references or supporting images instead of full-slide screenshots.
Regenerate only the weak slide or batch rather than rebuilding the full deck.
